Large savings vs oil and direct electric

settings.question: Is air-to-water as good as ground source?
settings.answer: Ground source has higher efficiency year-round, but air-to-water is cheaper to invest in and a great choice when drilling is not possible or practical.

settings.question: Which heat pump suits my home?
settings.answer: Air-to-water suits homes with water-based heating (radiators or underfloor). If you have direct electric heating without a water circuit, air-to-air is a better fit.

settings.question: How much can I save with a heat pump?
settings.answer: Most customers reduce their heating costs by 50–70%. The exact saving depends on house size, current heating type and local energy prices.

settings.question: Do I get ROT deduction on heat pump installation?
settings.answer: Yes, the labour part of the installation qualifies for a 30% ROT deduction. We handle the paperwork and deduct it directly from your invoice.

settings.badge: Heating and hot water in one system
settings.heading: Air-to-Water Heat Pump in Stockholm
settings.intro: An air-to-water heat pump takes heat from outdoor air and transfers it to the home's water-based heating system and tap water. A great alternative to ground source when drilling is not possible.
settings.suitable_for: Houses with water-based systems, oil boilers or direct electric heating with added culvert.
